name: Secrets Rotator
description: Audits and documents all CI/CD secrets required for the pipeline. ALL secret values are scrubbed before reaching the LLM — only key NAMES are processed. Produces a secrets management plan covering storage location, rotation frequency, minimal permissions, and job access scope. No actual secret values appear in any output.

checks.pass: repo-profile contract available — loading deployment target to determine required secrets
checks.fail: repo-profile contract not available — cannot assess secrets without knowing deployment target

checks.prompt: Produce a CI/CD secrets management plan. IMPORTANT: You are processing key NAMES only — all actual secret values have been scrubbed by the pipeline before this prompt. For each of the following secret NAMES, specify: (1) STORAGE LOCATION: repo secret (single-repo access), environment secret (specific environment only), or org secret (org-wide access) — apply principle of least privilege. (2) ROTATION FREQUENCY: recommended rotation interval with justification (e.g., API keys: 90 days, tokens: 30 days). (3) MINIMAL PERMISSIONS: exact required scopes or permissions (e.g., VERCEL_TOKEN: deployments:write only). (4) JOB ACCESS: which specific workflow jobs need this secret — do not give blanket access. Secrets to document: VERCEL_TOKEN, DOCKER_HUB_TOKEN, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, NPM_TOKEN. Output a structured secrets management plan with allSecretsDocumented:true and confirm noSecretValuesInOutput:true.
checks.pass: Secrets plan complete — all five secrets documented with storage, rotation, permissions, and job access scope
checks.fail: Secrets plan incomplete or security violation detected
